questions.prompt: What's the canonical production pattern for most SaaS apps?

choices.text: Run everything on Cloudflare or everything on AWS.

Hybrid: Cloudflare for edge (DNS, security, caching, sometimes Workers + R2), cloud provider for compute and primary DB. Each ecosystem for what it's best at. Pages or Workers for the frontend / lightweight backends; AWS / GCP / Vercel / Fly for heavy backend; Postgres on a managed provider. Lower total cost than all-one-provider in most real workloads.

questions.explanation: Hybrid is the right answer for most production SaaS. Cloudflare's edge strengths complement traditional cloud's compute/data strengths. All-in on one provider often costs more for less fit.

questions.prompt: You're adding Cloudflare to an existing production setup. What's the safe migration approach?

choices.text: Flip everything to orange-cloud at once with default settings.

Gradual: add Cloudflare DNS with records gray-cloud first, set SSL mode to Full (strict), install Cloudflare Origin Cert at origin, orange-cloud one record at a time with verification, enable WAF managed rules in Log mode, monitor for false positives, promote to Block over weeks. Lock origin firewall to Cloudflare IPs once stable.

questions.explanation: Gradual migration limits blast radius. Big-bang flips routinely cause outages — wrong SSL mode, mail breakage, WAF blocking real users. Record-by-record + Log-mode WAF gives you safe rollback at each step.
